name: Hearth
look: A steady amber fire keeps the room kind without making it sleepy.
search: The ashes have been swept into a small arrow pointing from the hearth toward the low doorway.

uses.text: The Hearth Tonic warms in your hand, but the hearth is already doing its gentlest work.

name: Low Doorway
look: The doorway is low enough that even brave guests bow before leaving.
search: Old scratch marks near the frame point toward Rain-Soft Garden, Homeroom, and a shadow small enough for Skull.

uses.text: The Watch Bell gives one felt chime. Skull's silence seems to approve of checking the threshold.

name: Scarf Basket
look: Blue wool curls over the basket rim like a quiet sentence waiting for a verb.
search: Under the top skein is a round notch exactly the size of a warm wooden button.

uses.text: The Story Button nestles into the notch. The scarf basket clicks once, as if Rati's next tale found its first word.

name: Rain Window
look: Rain threads down the glass in patient silver lines.
search: The droplets draw a route: garden first, trail second, school after the kettle.

name: Broad Leaves
look: Wide leaves hold rain in neat, trembling beads.
search: One leaf has been turned over recently. Its underside shines with button-bright dew.

uses.text: The Dewbright Button matches the leaf's shine and hums toward Gust.

name: Puddle Path
look: Small puddles reflect the cottage window and a sliver of moonlit trail.
search: The puddles are shallow, but their reflections make the trail look closer than it is.

name: Silver Milepost
look: A silvered post marks the trail without naming the distance.
search: Three scratches read like advice: test your guard, take what is earned, leave before pride gets loud.

name: Practice Circle
look: Moonlight makes a pale circle where Coach waits.
search: The dust shows careful footwork and no blood. This place is meant for practice, not cruelty.

uses.text: The Wolfprint Charm cools in your palm, pleased by the circle's restraint.

name: Missing Sun Bell
look: A clean arch waits where a noon bell should hang. Sunlight outlines the absence in gold.
search: The empty bracket hums downward, as if the bell fell through sky, sea, and certainty.

name: Drowned Bell
look: A white-gold bell rests in black water, its rim crusted with pearls and patient songs.
search: The bell has not gone silent. It has learned a lower note the Solar Temple never wrote down.

name: Abyssal Anvil
look: A cold anvil waits beside the impossible feast, polished by pronouncements and bad timing.
search: The anvil rings without being struck, as if every route here expects a witness before it becomes real.

name: Lab Bench
look: A bright bench holds clean questions, small instruments, and room for a second try.
search: A note under the bench says: observe first, listen second, revise always.

name: Root Voice
look: The roots braid through the soil like roads that decided to stay.
search: Root remembers every footstep that circled back and every traveler who pretended not to be lost.

name: Ring Voice
look: The trunk's rings press time into a patient spiral.
search: Ring remembers the years when the forest was less lonely and the paths still argued about names.

name: Leaf Voice
look: Leaves turn their small green faces toward the newest sound.
search: Leaf remembers what changed today, which is nearly always more than the rest of the tree admits.

name: Hollow Voice
look: A dark hollow opens in the trunk, quiet enough to make questions lower their voices.
search: Hollow remembers what nobody has asked yet. It is waiting for a better question.

uses.text: The Story Button clicks inside the hollow. Old Oak answers with a story it had kept for a kinder listener.

name: Failing Lantern
look: The inn's road lantern gutters blue whenever the north door opens.
search: Soot inside the cage forms three clean finger marks: lens, key, and oil.

name: Cold Lamp Post
look: The lamp is intact but cold, with white moths packed beneath its copper hood.
search: A loose pane conceals the Mothglass Lens and one tiny note reading: not all shadows are enemies.

uses.text: Through the Mothglass Lens, one set of keeper's footprints shines toward Saint Orra's Ruin.

name: Stone Lantern
look: The chapel's stone lantern has no wick, only a keyhole hidden under rainwater.
search: The Keeper's Brass Key rests in a hollow beneath the unlit bowl.

uses.text: The brass key turns in the stone and reveals Rowan's warning: the flame knows my shape too well.

name: Golden Oil Slick
look: A thread of gold floats untouched across the barrow water.
search: The trail ends at a stoppered flask of Dawn Oil wedged between two dry stones.

uses.text: The Dawn Oil burns without heat, and every false reflection in the water goes dark.

name: Great Lantern Lens
look: The tower lens is clear glass by daylight and a solid black mirror now.
search: Behind the lens, the Keeper's Ember waits in a socket shaped for a human hand.

uses.text: The Keeper's Brass Key opens the inner lens cage without waking the tower's old traps.
The Mothglass Lens reveals Rowan inside the shadow rather than replaced by it.
Dawn Oil draws a gold boundary between Rowan and the borrowed dark.
The Keeper's Ember seats in the great lens and the road lamps answer one by one.

name: Lamplit Courtyard
look: A sheltered courtyard holds a clay lamp, a manger-shaped feeding trough, and the hush of arriving travelers.
search: You find a cool corner where someone has left clean straw and a cup of water for the next guest.

name: Village Workshop
look: Wood shavings curl beside well-used tools in an open-fronted village workshop.
search: You notice that every repaired yoke bears the patient marks of hands adjusting it to a particular burden.

name: River Reeds
look: Reeds open and close with the river wind beside a bank worn smooth by many crossings.
search: Between the stones, new green shoots rise from silt left by the last high water.

name: Stone Water Jars
look: Several large stone jars wait in the shade of a feast courtyard.
search: The jars are cool to the touch, carefully cleaned, and placed where serving hands can reach them quickly.

name: Crowded Doorway
look: A basalt doorway opens onto a room where neighbors have made space by standing shoulder to shoulder.
search: Scuffed stone at the threshold shows how many people have arrived carrying someone else's hope.

name: Drying Nets
look: Nets hang from a rough rack, glittering with lake water and threaded with careful repairs.
search: A torn mesh has been marked with a bright cord so nobody forgets where patient work must begin.

name: Listening Stone
look: A broad flat stone faces the lake from the grassy slope, warmed by the afternoon sun.
search: From here the nearest voice carries clearly without needing to shout over the whole hillside.

name: Boat Yard
look: A half-repaired fishing boat rests on wooden braces above the reed line.
search: Under the hull, a basket holds pegs, cord, and one place left open for the tool that has gone missing.

name: Northern Spring
look: Clear cold water emerges beneath the limestone cliff and gathers into a swift channel.
search: The spring reflects the cliff and sky together, making it difficult to say where one surface ends.

name: Old Stone Well
look: Deep-worn stones circle a well beside the Samaritan road, offering a small island of shade.
search: Grooves in the rim remember generations of ropes, jars, and hands drawing water for strangers and kin.

name: Roadside Shade
look: A date palm throws a narrow strip of shade beside the climbing road.
search: The dust holds many footprints, but one clear space remains where the crowd has learned to make room.

name: Table of Friends
look: Bread, herbs, and clay cups wait on a low table in a house prepared for friends.
search: An extra place has been set without asking who might arrive to need it.

name: Pilgrim Gate
look: Travelers, animals, merchants, and families pass beneath a limestone gate toward the festival city.
search: Among the noise, you hear many accents sharing the same brief prayers for a safe arrival.

name: Old Olive Tree
look: A furrowed olive trunk divides into patient branches beneath the night sky.
search: The roots hold the rocky ground with a strength that looks almost like kneeling.

name: Unmarked Mile Stone
look: A weathered roadside stone gives travelers a place to rest without telling them how far remains.
search: Three sets of recent footprints overlap in the dust, keeping company for part of the westward road.
